claim: MCP servers that provide external knowledge (like documentation retrieval) are essential for AI coding workflows to improve code quality and reduce errors.
actionable: Integrate a RAG-based MCP server like Crawl4AI into your Claude Code configuration to automatically fetch and cache library documentation.
source_title: The 3 MUST Have MCP Servers for Any AI Coding (and How to Use Them)

claim: Database MCP servers like Supabase enable AI agents to autonomously create tables, write SQL queries, and manage schema during application development without manual database configuration.
actionable: Configure the Supabase MCP server in Windsurf/Cursor and prompt the AI to use the apply_migration tool to handle all database schema creation in natural language.
source_title: The 3 MUST Have MCP Servers for Any AI Coding (and How to Use Them)

claim: Web search MCP servers (like Brave Search) complement RAG-based knowledge bases by finding supplemental resources like forum posts and community examples that documentation may miss.
actionable: Use Brave MCP server alongside your documentation RAG server to fill knowledge gaps with real-world usage examples from forums and Stack Overflow.
source_title: The 3 MUST Have MCP Servers for Any AI Coding (and How to Use Them)

claim: Using concrete examples from your own projects in prompts significantly improves AI's ability to build similar code patterns correctly without iteration.
actionable: Reference existing code files in your prompts when building new features; e.g., 'Here's how I built streaming in streamlit before, use this as a reference.'
source_title: The 3 MUST Have MCP Servers for Any AI Coding (and How to Use Them)

claim: MCP servers require detailed README documentation with step-by-step setup instructions to be truly usable by both human developers and other AI systems.
actionable: When creating an MCP server, include setup walkthroughs covering configuration, dependency installation, credential setup, and integration with at least Cursor, Windsurf, and Claude Code.
source_title: The 3 MUST Have MCP Servers for Any AI Coding (and How to Use Them)

claim: MCP server configuration in modern AI IDEs follows a standardized JSON format across Windsurf, Cursor, and Claude Code, allowing configuration reuse between tools.
actionable: Create MCP configurations that are tool-agnostic by following the standard JSON format, allowing you to switch between Claude Code and Cursor without reconfiguration.
source_title: The 3 MUST Have MCP Servers for Any AI Coding (and How to Use Them)

claim: Instructing the AI to fetch documentation from MCP servers early in the development process, before code generation, leads to more accurate implementations with fewer bugs.
actionable: Add explicit instructions in your prompt like 'Use MCP servers to fetch Pydantic and Supabase docs BEFORE writing any code' to ensure knowledge is available during implementation.
source_title: The 3 MUST Have MCP Servers for Any AI Coding (and How to Use Them)

claim: Brave Search API offers a free tier and an AI-optimized web search that returns better results than ChatGPT's built-in search for tool usage and integration patterns.
actionable: Set up Brave MCP server using their free tier API to supplement knowledge-base searches with up-to-date web results for your AI coding workflows.
source_title: The 3 MUST Have MCP Servers for Any AI Coding (and How to Use Them)
